metadata.description: A JavaScript research dashboard that imports all workbook responses, shows charts, collects new notes, and exports Excel-readable files.

players.Name: Armani Bradley
players.Research Use: Story/detail-oriented; used character familiarity to connect with Spider-Man.

players.Name: Christopher Fowls
players.Research Use: Mechanics-first; movement, feel, and control matter more than story/readability.

gameSummaries.Interpretation: Peak: emotion + narrative integration; Risk: slower scavenging sections
Peak: combat weight + father/son stakes; Risk: puzzle pacing
Peak: cinematic exploration + world beauty; Risk: travel fatigue
Peak: machine combat + world mystery; Risk: resource/crafting friction
Peak: movement freedom + instant joy; Risk: side activity repetition
Peak: set pieces + character banter; Risk: linear pacing dips
Peak: weapon variety + spectacle; Risk: lower emotional weight
Peak: challenge loop + replay structure; Risk: difficulty fatigue
Peak: atmosphere + unique world; Risk: slow traversal and niche loop
Peak: mechanical mastery + progression; Risk: weaker narrative pull

responses.Player Note: The opening felt tense and emotional while the player learned the controls. I needed time to understand the controls, so I moved slower at the beginning.
The opening felt tense and emotional while the player learned the controls. I needed time to understand the controls, so I moved slower at the beginning.

quizQuestions.Question: During the 40 minutes, how immersed did you feel overall?
What pulled you into the game the most?
Did your actions feel like they mattered?
How often did the game make you want to keep playing?
What made you feel tired or disconnected?
Did the world feel believable?
quizQuestions.Choice E: Believable because of story and environment

quizQuestions.Question: What moment was the strongest immersion point?
Would you keep playing after the 40 minutes ended?
quizQuestions.Choice D: Only if I had more time
quizQuestions.Choice E: Yes, mainly to see what happens next
